HGSS.381.0000:
Excuse me, kid!
I received this letter the other day\r
from a friend of mine.\f
Here’s the reply that I wrote.\f
I am stuck here and was wondering
if you could deliver it...\f
He’s on Route 31.\r
Can you help a guy out? [0200,0000]
HGSS.381.0001:
Oh... Never mind, then...
You must be busy, too.
HGSS.381.0005:
My friend is on Route 31.\f
He looks obviously eager to read
letters, so you’ll know who he is\r
right away!
HGSS.381.0006:
My friend is on Route 31.\f
Oh, yeah.
There was a weird tree\r
blocking the road.\r
I wonder if it’s been cleared?
HGSS.381.0007:
Thank you for making the delivery!\f
...What about a cave exploration?
Ah, you can go right through here.\f
I want you to have this for
your kindness!\f
HGSS.381.0008:
My pal was snoozing, right?
Heh, he’s always sleeping.
HGSS.381.0009:
What?!
You lost the Mail?\r
Oh, well. That’s OK.\r
Don’t worry about it.
HGSS.381.0011:
A strange tree is blocking the road.
It wriggles around if you talk to it.\f
I heard it became wild when someone
watered it with a SquirtBottle.
HGSS.381.0012:
I like the Pokémon Lullaby they play
on the radio.
HGSS.381.0013:
I can’t believe such a tiny change
turns the Pokégear into a radio!\f
Oh, your Pokégear doesn’t have
that feature yet?\f
It’s a shame!\f
They happen to give quizzes at the
Radio Tower reception on the\r
first floor.\f
If you can answer them correctly,
they’ll give you the Radio Card!\f
You better try it while they
have some left!
HGSS.381.0014:
I may not look it, but I’m pretty good
at mechanics.\f
Do you want to know how to
listen to the radio? [0200,0000]
HGSS.381.0015:
You open your Pokégear and press the
radio button. Then you see the screen\r
for tuning in a signal.\f
The middle of the screen is the
radio-reception map.\f
You can move the blinking mark on the
map around to tune in to different\r
radio shows.\f
For those who think this is a pain,
there are four channels, two on each\r
side, for easy tuning.
HGSS.381.0016: Was I being a backseat driver?
